body.title: Granny: GRANNY_DISPATCH posted twice per dispatch event
body.result: push_ws=False in daemon._post_channel — root cause was stale web server ignoring ws_only; 3 new regression tests
body.intention: I intend that each Granny dispatch event produces exactly one GRANNY_DISPATCH channel message.
body.description:
Every time Granny dispatches a ticket, GRANNY_DISPATCH appears twice in the shared channel with identical content and the same timestamp. Observed live: 2026-06-04T18:10:06 showed two identical posts for T-ibd-intention-field and T-too-intentions-annotation.

The double-post likely comes from _post_channel() being called twice, or from the channel implementation writing twice. The _dispatch_cc0 and _dispatch_dicksimnel functions each call _post_channel() once in run_once() — need to check if there is a second call path.

**Affected files:** devices/granny/daemon.py (run_once, _post_channel), unseen_university/channel.py
**Design rules:** no-sqlite N/A; log-every-interface-crossing: channel post IS the log
**Scope boundary:** fix the double-post; do not change dispatch logic or message format
**Completion criteria:** channel observation after fix shows exactly one GRANNY_DISPATCH per dispatch event; test asserts _post_channel called once per run_once dispatch
